programming: OBD programmer required (Autel, Lonsdor, OBDSTAR). Some older units allow on-board.
notes: HY22 blade used on US market; HY20/LXP90 for Canadian/Touring variant. Chip is cloneable ID46.

immobilizer: dual-option era (DST80 or PCF7952A depending on trim/build date)
programming: PIN required. PIN-by-VIN calculable via DiagCode (2006–2016). OBD programmer required.
notes: 2011-2015 US Elantra uses DST80 or PCF7952A depending on trim. OEM FCC SY5HMFNA04 at 315 MHz covers Elantra/Sonata/Genesis era. PIN-by-VIN available 2006-2016.

immobilizer: Texas AES 128-bit (ID8A) on push-button trims; non-transponder flip key on turn-key trims
programming: OBD programmer required for Smart key. Flip key: program via OBD (remote only, no transponder).
notes: TWO key types by trim: push-button-start trims (Sport/Value/SE w/PKG) use Smart key FCC CQOFD00120 (8A chip, 434 MHz, PN 95440-F2000 for 2016-18 / 95440-F2002 for 2019-20); standard-ignition SE trims (2016-2018) use Flip key FCC OKA-NO38 (non-transponder, 434 MHz, PN 95430-F2001). Elantra GT Sport 2018-2020 uses FCC NYOSYEC4FOB1608. Emergency key uses HY22 blade on smart variant; HYN14R/LXP90 is the flip blade.

programming: OBD programmer required. No on-board procedure. Professional locksmith or dealer.
notes: 2021+ Elantra uses 6A HITAG-AES chip. FCC NYOMBEC5FOB2004, PN 95440-AA000 (5-btn trunk) or 95440-AA100 (4-btn). Emergency blade KK12. 433 MHz confirmed by OEM Amazon listing and multiple locksmith suppliers.

programming: OBD programmer or on-board (some older). Cloneable with ID46 cloner.
notes: 2005-2010 Sonata uses ID46 PCF7936AS with HY22/LXP90 blade.

programming: PIN required. PIN-by-VIN (2006-2016). OBD programmer. Smart key must be cut by locksmith.
notes: 2011-2014 uses FCC SY5HMFNA04 at 315 MHz. PCF7952A chip. OEM PNs: 95440-3Q000, 95440-3M220. PIN-by-VIN era.

immobilizer: Texas AES 128-bit (ID8A) on push-button trims; non-transponder on turn-key trims
programming: OBD programmer required for Smart key. Flip key: remote programming only, no transponder chip.
notes: TWO key types by trim: push-button-start trims (SE/Sport/Limited) use Smart key FCC CQOFD00120 (8A chip, 434 MHz, PNs 95440-C1000/C1001 for 2015-17, 95440-C1500/C2500 for 2018-19); standard-ignition trims use a non-transponder Flip key — FCC TQ8-RKE-4F16 (PN 95430-C1010, 433 MHz, KK10 blade, 2015-2017) or FCC TQ8-RKE-4F25 (PN 95430-C1210/C2210, 433 MHz, LXP90 blade, 2017-2019). Verify by VIN/FCC on fob.

programming: OBD programmer required. No on-board. Programmable by Xhorse, Lonsdor, OBDSTAR, Autel.
notes: 2020-2021 uses TOY49 emergency blade (5-btn variant L1010 at 434 MHz; 7-btn variant L1500/L1600 at 433 MHz); 2022+ shifted to KK12. FCC 4F27 = 5-btn at 434 MHz, FCC 4F28 = 7-btn at 433 MHz. Multiple part numbers — verify VIN.

programming: OBD programmer or cloner for ID46.
notes: First-gen Tucson 2004-2009 uses standard ID46 PCF7936.

programming: PIN required (2006-2016 era). PIN-by-VIN. OBD programmer.
notes: 2010-2015 Tucson 2nd gen. PCF7952A/Hitag2 chip. FCC SY5HMFNA04 at 315 MHz. PN 95440-2S300.

immobilizer: HITAG3 / ID47 on push-button trims; non-transponder on turn-key trims
programming: OBD programmer required for Smart key. Flip key: remote programming only, no transponder chip.
notes: TWO key types by trim: push-button-start trims use Smart key — FCC TQ8-FOB-4F07 (PN 95440-D3100, 2016-2018) or FCC TQ8-FOB-4F11 (PN 95440-D3110, 2018-2020), ID47 HITAG3 at 433 MHz; standard-ignition trims use Flip key FCC TQ8-RKE-4F25 (PN 95430-D3010/D3000, 433 MHz, KK10/LXP90 blade, non-transponder, 2015-2021 US). HY22 emergency blade on smart variants. Chip corrected from DST80→ID47 HITAG3 per multiple live sources.

programming: OBD programmer required. Professional locksmith or dealer.
notes: 4th gen Tucson 2021+. FCC TQ8-FOB-4F26. TOY49 emergency blade confirmed by keyshop-online OEM listing and abkeys. OEM PNs: 95440-N9030 (2021, 4-btn), 95440-N9050/N9052 (2022-24, 4-btn), 95440-N9070/N9072 (5-btn). Frequency: abkeys genuine listing confirms 433 MHz for PN N9030; keyshop-online OEM N9030 lists 434 MHz — conflict; Keyless2Go (N9052) confirms 433 MHz. Listing as 433 MHz (2 sources vs 1). Note: some 2025+ models (ID4A/KK12) are a separate gen.

programming: OBD programmer or ID46 cloner.
notes: Santa Fe 2007-2012 uses ID46 PCF7936. Precoded on some 2012 dealer stock.

immobilizer: PCF7952A / Hitag2 on push-button trims; non-transponder on turn-key trims
programming: PIN required for Smart key (2006-2016 era, PIN-by-VIN). OBD programmer. Flip key: remote programming only, no transponder.
notes: TWO key types by trim: push-button-start trims use Smart key FCC SY5HMFNA04 (PCF7952A, 315 MHz, PN 95440-3V021); standard-ignition trims use Flip key — FCC TQ8-RKE-3F04 (PN 95430-4Z101/4Z100, 315 MHz, HY18R blade, non-transponder, 2013-2016) or FCC TQ8-RKE-4F31 (PN 95430-2W110, 433 MHz, HY18R blade, non-transponder, 2017-2018). Later 2016+ units may have HiTag3 on Smart variant.

programming: OBD programmer required. Professional locksmith or dealer.
notes: 2019-2020 uses FCC TQ8-FOB-4F19, PN 95440-S2000, 434 MHz. 2021-2024 uses FCC TQ8-FOB-4F27/4F28, PN 95440-S1670/S1660. KK12 emergency blade. ID47 chip across all.

programming: OBD programmer or ID46 cloner.
notes: 2012-2017 Accent uses HY18 keyway (distinct from HY22 used on larger models). ID46 chip cloneable.

programming: OBD programmer required for transponder variant (J0800). Base flip (J0700) has no transponder.
notes: 2018-2022 Accent uses flip key FCC NYOSYEC4TX1707, KK12 blade, 434 MHz. Two PN variants: 95430-J0700 (non-transponder, no chip) and 95430-J0800 (with Texas 4D60 80-bit transponder). Chip corrected from 'Philips ID47/HITAG3' (wrong) → non-transponder / Texas 4D60 per Locksmith Keyless, UHS Hardware, Transponder Island. Part number corrected from 95440-J0700→95430-J0700.

programming: OBD programmer required.
notes: Kona 2018-2020. ID47 HITAG3 chip. KK12 emergency blade. 433 MHz. OEM PNs J9000/J9100.

programming: OBD programmer required. Programmable by Lonsdor, OBDSTAR, Autel.
notes: 2021-2022 Kona uses ID47 NCF29A1X at 433 MHz (FCC TQ8-FOB-4F43, PN J9450/J9600). Some base trim models may use transponder-only with KK12/4D60. 2024+ Kona shifts to ID4A chip.

programming: OBD programmer required. Professional locksmith or dealer.
notes: Palisade 2020-2021 uses FCC TQ8-FOB-4F29, PN 95440-S8010, 5-btn at 433 MHz. 2022+ switches to FCC TQ8-FOB-4F28 (7-btn, PN S8590/S8600). ID47 NCF29A1X.

programming: OBD programmer required.
OBD programmer required.
notes: 2012-2018 Veloster uses Texas DST80/ID6E-MA. HY22 emergency blade.

programming: OBD programmer required. Smart Pro (Ilco) or Autel.
notes: 2019-2022 Veloster and Veloster N use FCC SY5IGFGE04 at 434 MHz. ID47 HITAG3. PN 95440-J3000 (Veloster) / K9000 (Veloster N). KK12 emergency blade.

immobilizer: HITAG3 / ID47 or HITAG-AES 4A depending on trim
programming: OBD programmer required. Lonsdor, OBDSTAR, Autel.
notes: Venue 2020-2024 uses FCC SY5IGFGE04 at 434 MHz, PN 95440-K2400/K2410. Some base trims use transponder-only KK12 with 4A chip. KK12 emergency blade.

programming: OBD programmer or ID46 cloner.
notes: 2001-2010 Optima/Magentis uses ID46. KK10 blade.

programming: PIN required (PIN-by-VIN 2006-2016). OBD programmer.
notes: 2011-2015 Optima. PCF7952A. FCC SY5HMFNA04 at 315 MHz. KK10 or KK12 blade. OEM also lists SVI-HMFNA04, SVI-XMFGE03.

programming: OBD programmer required.
notes: 2016-2020 Optima uses HITAG3. OEM PN 95440-D4100. KK12 blade.

programming: OBD programmer required. Autel IM608, Lonsdor.
notes: K5 (renamed from Optima) 2021-2023. 4A chip, FCC CQOFD00790, PN 95440-L3020/L3010, 433 MHz. KK12 emergency blade.

programming: PIN required (PIN-by-VIN). OBD programmer.
notes: 2008-2013 Forte (Cerato) uses PCF7952A with FCC SVI-HMFNA04 at 315 MHz. KK10 blade.

immobilizer: Texas DST80 on push-button trims; non-transponder on turn-key trims
programming: OBD programmer required for Smart key. Flip key: remote programming only, no transponder chip.
notes: TWO key types by trim: push-button-start trims (EX/SX) use Smart key with DST80 chip, KK12 blade; standard-ignition trims (LX/FE) use a non-transponder Flip key — FCC OSLOKA-870T (PN 95430-A7400, 315 MHz, HY15 blade, 2014-2016), FCC OSLOKA-875T (PN 95430-A7200, 433 MHz, HY15 blade, 2017-2018), or FCC CQOTD00660 (PN 95430-M6400/M6000, 434 MHz, KK12 blade, 2019-2020). Covers Forte, Forte5, Forte Koup.

programming: OBD programmer required. Autel, Lonsdor.
notes: 2021-2023 Forte transponder key uses 8A Texas AES with KK12 blade. Smart key variant uses 4A chip, FCC CQOFD00790. Forte shares key blank with Seltos/K5.

programming: PIN required (PIN-by-VIN). OBD programmer.
notes: 1st gen Soul 2008-2013. PCF7952A Hitag2. FCC SVI-HMFNA04 at 315 MHz. KK10 blade.

programming: OBD programmer required.
notes: 2nd gen Soul 2014-2018. Texas DST80/ID6E-MA. OEM PN 95430-B2100. KK12 blade.

programming: OBD programmer required. Lonsdor, Autel, OBDSTAR.
notes: 3rd gen Soul 2019-2023. 4A HITAG-AES chip. KK12 blade. Smart key and transponder variants. Soul shares key family with Sorento 2019-2024.

programming: PIN required (PIN-by-VIN). OBD programmer.
notes: 2010-2014 Sorento. PCF7952A. FCC SVI-HMFNA04, 315 MHz. KK10 blade.

programming: OBD programmer required.
notes: TWO keys by trim: push-button-start trims use the SMART key (TQ8-FOB-4F06); turn-key-ignition trims use the FLIP key (OSLOKA-910T, PN 95430-C5101). Both ID47/HITAG3, 433 MHz, KK12 emergency blade. Confirm by the FCC printed on the fob.

programming: OBD programmer required. Professional only.
notes: 2021-2023 Sorento 4th gen. 4A HITAG-AES (NCF29A1M). FCC SY5MQ4FGE05 (5-btn, PN P2010) confirmed 433 MHz by abkeys genuine OEM listing and Amazon KEYECU listing — corrected from erroneous 434 MHz. FCC SY5MQ4AFGE04 (4-btn, PN R5010), SY5SKRGE03 (flip 3-btn, PN 95430-R5100). All use KK12 blade.

programming: OBD programmer or ID46 cloner.
notes: 2004-2009 Sportage. ID46 PCF7936. KK10 blade.

programming: PIN required (PIN-by-VIN). OBD programmer.
notes: 2010-2016 Sportage. PCF7952A. FCC SVI-HMFNA04 at 315 MHz. KK10 blade. OEM PN 95440-3W350.

immobilizer: HITAG3 / ID47 on push-button trims; non-transponder on turn-key trims
programming: OBD programmer required for Smart key. Flip key: remote programming only, no transponder chip.
notes: TWO key types by trim: push-button-start trims (EX/SX/SX Turbo) use Smart key FCC TQ8-FOB-4F08 (ID47 HITAG3, 433 MHz, PN 95440-D9500, KK12 blade); standard-ignition trims (LX/LX Turbo) use Flip key FCC TQ8-RKE-4F27 (non-transponder, 434 MHz, PN 95430-D9100/D9000, KK10 blade, 2016-2020). US market only — Canadian Sportage flip key requires transponder chip (different PN).

programming: OBD programmer required.
notes: 2022+ Sportage NQ5 gen. 4A chip. KK12 blade. Transponder key also available for base trim. Shares key with Sorento 2021+.

programming: OBD programmer required. Professional locksmith or dealer.
notes: Telluride 2020-2022. FCC TQ8-FOB-4F24 (4-btn, PN S9000) and TQ8-FOB-4F34 (5-btn, PN S9200/S9330). ID47 PCF7938Xa. 433 MHz. KK12 emergency blade.

programming: OBD programmer required.
notes: 2023-2025 Telluride (facelift). FCC TQ8-FOB-4F71. 433 MHz. KK12 blade. Chip is ID47 HITAG3 (not 4A AES as previously listed — multiple live sources confirm HITAG3). PNs: S9510 (5-btn), S9540 (5-btn alt), S9550 (4-btn).

programming: OBD programmer or cloner.
notes: 2006-2011 Rio. ID46. KK10 blade.

immobilizer: PCF7952 / Hitag2 on SX push-button trims; non-transponder on LX/EX turn-key trims
programming: OBD programmer required for Smart key. Flip key: remote programming only, no transponder chip.
notes: TWO key types by trim: SX (push-button-start) uses Smart key FCC SVI-MDFEU03 (PCF7952 Hitag2, 433 MHz, PN 95430-H8600); LX/EX (standard ignition) use Flip key — FCC TQ8-RKE-3F02 (PN 95430-1W020/1W021, 315 MHz, KK10/LXP90 blade, non-transponder, 2012-2013) or FCC TQ8-RKE-3F05 (PN 95430-1W023/1W003, 315 MHz, LXP90 blade, non-transponder, 2014-2017). SX push-button-start was Premium Package option 2012-2015, standard on SX from 2016.

programming: OBD programmer required. Smart Pro (Ilco) or Autel.
notes: 2018-2023 Rio. Flip key with FCC NYOSYEC4TX1611, PN 95430-H9850. Texas 4D+70 DST80 chip. KK12 blade. 433 MHz.

programming: OBD programmer required. Lonsdor, OBDSTAR, Autel.
notes: Seltos 2021-2023. Smart key FCC NYOSYEC5FOB1907, PN Q5400 (2021-22) / Q5410 (2022-24). 6A NXP AES chip. Transponder-only trim uses 8A chip. KK12 blade.

programming: OBD programmer or cloner.
notes: Sedona 2002-2014 (Carnival in other markets). ID46. HY22/LXP90 blade for some; also KK10 for some years.

programming: OBD programmer required.
notes: Sedona 2015-2020 / Carnival 2021 uses FCC SVI-YPFGE05, PN 95440-A9200/A9300, 433 MHz, HITAG3 ID47. HY22 emergency blade.

programming: OBD programmer required.
notes: 2022-2024 Carnival (US Sedona replacement). HITAG-AES 4A chip. KK12 blade. 434 MHz. FCC SY5KA4FGE06 = 6-btn (PN R0410); SY5KA4FGE07 = 7-btn (PN R0420). Both confirmed by locksmithkeyless + UHS Hardware.

programming: PIN required (PIN-by-VIN). OBD programmer.
notes: 2009-2013 Genesis Sedan (Hyundai-badged). PCF7952A. FCC SY5HMFNA04 at 315 MHz. PIN-by-VIN era.

programming: OBD programmer required. Professional locksmith only. No on-board.
notes: G70 2019-2021. FCC TQ8-FOB-4F16. PN 95440-G9000. ID47 HITAG3. 433 MHz. KK12 emergency blade. Also covers G90 2019-2020 (same key).

programming: OBD programmer required. Professional only.
notes: G70 2022-2024 (refresh). FCC TQ8-FOB-4F36. PN 95440-G9530 (6-btn trunk) / G9630. 433 MHz. ID47 HITAG3. KK12 blade.

programming: OBD programmer required. Professional only.
notes: G80 2017-2021 (Genesis-branded). FCC TQ8-FOB-4F35. PN 95440-B1210. ID47. 433 MHz. KK12 blade.

programming: OBD programmer required. Professional only.
notes: G80 2022-2025. FCC TQ8-FOB-4F36. PN 95440-T1010. HITAG3 ID47. 434 MHz (corrected from erroneous 315 MHz; confirmed by locksmithkeyless + UHS Hardware). 6-btn. KK12 blade. Also 8-btn variant TQ8-FOB-4F53 PN T1210.

programming: OBD programmer required. Professional only.
notes: GV80 2020-2024. FCC TQ8-FOB-4F35. Primary OEM PNs: 95440-T6011 (2021, 6-btn), 95440-T6014 (2022+, 8-btn), 95440-T6100 (later). Earlier PN 95440-T6010 may be pre-production or 2020 variant. ID47 HITAG3. 433 MHz confirmed (abkeys, keyinnovations). KK12 emergency blade. Also shares FCC with G80 2017-2021 (different PN family).
